title: A resolution concerning the National Collegiate Athletic Association policy for eligibility in women's sports.
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
plain_summary: This Senate resolution asks the National Collegiate Athletic Association to change its current rules about transgender athletes. Right now, the NCAA allows some transgender students to compete on women's sports teams. The resolution wants the NCAA to create new rules that only allow people who were born female to play on women's teams. The senators who support this resolution believe it would make sports more fair and safer for female athletes.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Resolution Concerning the National Collegiate Athletic Association Policy for Eligibility in Women's Sports
sections.text: This resolution is about rules for who can play on women's college sports teams. The Senate states several important facts. Playing sports has a positive impact on young girls by improving their physical health, emotions, mental health, self-confidence, and discipline. Women have achieved some of the greatest athletic accomplishments in United States sports history, from the Olympic games to professional competition, through opportunities to compete in college sports. In nineteen seventy-two, Congress passed a law called Title IX that was a turning point in federal support for women in sports. This law applies to almost all colleges and universities that receive federal money. There are basic and lasting biological differences between males and females that put females at a disadvantage when competing in sports against males and can make competition unsafe for females. In two thousand ten, the National Collegiate Athletic Association (a group that oversees college sports) made a policy that allows biological males to participate on women's teams and compete in women's sports categories. This policy is still in place today. This policy hurts female athletes more than others. The National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics has created new policies to protect biological women in sports and ensure that only student athletes whose biological sex is female can compete on their women's sports teams. It is very important that opportunities for college women to compete in athletics are protected based on sex. Member schools of the National Collegiate Athletic Association must follow Title IX to ensure equal benefits and opportunities in athletic programs based on sex. Therefore, the Senate calls on the National Collegiate Athletic Association to cancel its transgender student-athlete eligibility policy that discriminates against female student athletes. The Senate asks the National Collegiate Athletic Association to immediately protect the integrity of college women's sports by not allowing transgender-identifying males to compete on any women's sports roster or in any college competition. The Senate urges the National Collegiate Athletic Association to require its member conferences to follow a biological sex-based policy across all sports and all divisions. The Senate calls on all sports-governing bodies in the United States to protect the category of women's sport for biological women and girls.
